description: Rubric for the Bookmarks baseline seed. Concrete, checkable assertions about what a correct /weave run should and should not produce, given the seed text and the canned answer queue (flat list, reject duplicates, no search, immutable, newest-first). Authored as a documented stub; no automated grader consumes it yet.

expected_behavior.assertion: A bookmark can be saved with a URL and a title.
expected_behavior.source: seed: 'I can save a URL with a title.'

expected_behavior.assertion: All saved bookmarks are shown in one list.
expected_behavior.source: seed: 'I can see all my saved bookmarks in one list.'

expected_behavior.assertion: A saved bookmark can be opened in a new tab.
expected_behavior.source: seed: 'I can open a saved bookmark in a new tab.'
seed: 'I can delete a bookmark I no longer want.'

expected_behavior.assertion: Bookmarks are a flat list with no tags and no categories.
expected_behavior.source: answers Q01: 'Flat list (no tags / no categories)'

expected_behavior.assertion: Saving a URL that already exists is rejected with an inline error; duplicates are neither merged nor silently allowed.
expected_behavior.source: answers Q02: 'Reject duplicate URLs with an inline error'

expected_behavior.assertion: There is no search box; the UI is a chronological list only.
Bookmarks are immutable once added; there is no edit-title or edit-URL capability.
Bookmarks are sorted newest-first only; no alternate sort order is offered.
The five deferred decisions were surfaced as Spec questions (consumed from the canned answer queue), not silently chosen by the agent.
expected_behavior.source: seed: 'Things I have not decided yet and want you to actually ask me about, not silently choose'

expected_behavior.assertion: TypeScript is used everywhere; no substitutions.
Backend is Node + Express, single process.
expected_behavior.source: seed stack: 'Backend: Node + Express, single process.'

expected_behavior.assertion: Storage is SQLite via better-sqlite3, a file on disk next to the server.
expected_behavior.source: seed stack: 'Storage: SQLite via better-sqlite3, file on disk next to the server.'

expected_behavior.assertion: Frontend is plain HTML + CSS + vanilla TypeScript bundled with esbuild; no React, Vue, or any framework.
expected_behavior.source: seed stack: 'plain HTML + CSS + vanilla TypeScript (compiled to one JS bundle via esbuild). No React, no Vue, no framework.'

expected_behavior.assertion: npm start boots the server on http://localhost:3000 and serves the UI from the same origin; npm test runs the tests. Both runnable from ./app/.

All deliverable files (package.json, tsconfig.json, source, tests, build output, node_modules, the SQLite file) are created inside ./app/ within the .loom/<project>/ workspace; nothing is written to the repo root, orchestrator/, or a sibling workspace.
expected_behavior.source: harness directive at top of baseline-seed.md; spec.md ## Constraints must surface it

expected_behavior.assertion: No nice-to-haves beyond the four requested features. Specifically no telemetry, no analytics, no service worker, no PWA manifest, and no dark-mode toggle (unless dark mode falls out of CSS for free).
expected_behavior.source: seed: 'Keep the surface small ... No nice-to-haves I did not ask for. No telemetry, no analytics, no service worker, no PWA manifest, no dark mode toggle unless it falls out of CSS for free.'

expected_behavior.assertion: Single user, local-only, no authentication, no deploy machinery.
expected_behavior.source: seed: 'Single user, runs on my laptop, no auth, no deploy.'
